ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00KY (Cocktails and mixed drinks) is the closest defensible category; no lychee-specific cocktail code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for lychee highball cocktail identified; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (one glass), reflecting a typical bar or restaurant pour of a lychee highball cocktail in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Lychee Highball — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Lychee Highball (cr_id G00011058), confidence 0.95. All per-100g macro and micronutrient values are taken verbatim from the HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — lychee and mixed lychee beverages analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a lychee highball cocktail in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 57 (medium) based on the GI of lychee fruit (GI ~57, University of Sydney GI Database) as the dominant carbohydrate contributor. Alcohol in the highball may modestly suppress postprandial glucose response, but the high sugar content (15.23 g/100g) from lychee syrup dominates. GL per serving calculated as: GI (57) × carbohydrate per serving (41.3 g per 250 ml) / 100 = 23.6, classified as high GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Alcohol content raises blood pressure acutely and chronically; sugar at 15.23 g/100g far exceeds the 5 g/100g sugar-sweetened beverage threshold
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid lychee highball entirely — alcohol directly elevates blood pressure and interacts with antihypertensive medications; a mocktail version using fresh lychee and unsweetened soda water is the recommended substitute.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (15.23 g/100g) triggers the sugar-sweetened beverage hard gate; alcohol impairs hepatic glucose regulation and interacts with diabetes medications including sulfonylureas and insulin
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid lychee highball — the combination of concentrated lychee syrup sugar and alcohol causes rapid glycaemic excursions and unpredictable hypoglycaemia risk, particularly when taken with glucose-lowering medications.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides, arrhythmia risk, and cardiomyopathy; high sugar load contributes to dyslipidaemia and metabolic syndrome
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid lychee highball — regular alcohol intake raises triglycerides and blood pressure, and the high sugar content (15.23 g/100g) promotes dyslipidaemia; a non-alcoholic lychee soda with minimal added sugar is the recommended alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Alcohol is pro-inflammatory and interacts with RA medications including methotrexate and biologics; high sugar further promotes systemic inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid lychee highball — alcohol directly promotes inflammation and is contraindicated with methotrexate and biologic therapies; request an alcohol-free lychee mocktail to eliminate both risks.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lychee Highball is a chilled alcoholic mixed beverage popular in Singapore, typically served in a tall glass over ice. It is prepared by combining a spirit base (commonly vodka or gin) with lychee syrup or canned lychee juice and topped with soda water, producing a light, effervescent drink with a distinctly sweet, floral, and tropical flavour profile characteristic of lychee fruit. The drink is visually appealing and often garnished with a whole lychee fruit. Per 100 g, it provides 66 kcal, 16.53 g carbohydrate (of which 15.23 g is sugar), 0.83 g protein, 0.44 g fat, 1.3 g dietary fibre, and 1 mg sodium, with notable potassium (171 mg) and magnesium (10 mg) from the lychee component. The high sugar content (15.23 g/100g) classifies this as a sugar-sweetened alcoholic beverage, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. Alcohol content further disqualifies it for all four conditions due to direct pharmacological interactions with common medications. The glycaemic index is estimated at 57 (medium) based on lychee fruit GI, but the glycaemic load per standard 250 ml serving is high at approximately 23.6. In Singapore, lychee highball is served at bars, restaurants, and social gatherings, and is associated with Malay and pan-Asian dining occasions. A healthier substitute is a lychee mocktail using fresh lychee and unsweetened soda water, which eliminates alcohol and substantially reduces sugar load.
common_user_queries: Is lychee highball suitable for diabetics?
Can I drink lychee highball if I have high blood pressure?
How many calories in a lychee cocktail?
What is the sugar content of lychee highball?
What alcohol is in a lychee highball?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00011058) with a reported confidence of 0.95 and review status 'reviewed'. The HPB is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. All core macros and key micronutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using lychee fruit as the closest defensible anchor, which is clearly documented.
notes: saturated_fat_g is reported as 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source; lychee highball is a low-fat beverage with negligible saturated fat by formulation. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source; this is a plant-based alcoholic beverage with no animal-derived fat components. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, full vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific lychee highball preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (G00011058) as of 2026. GI is an analogous estimate based on lychee fruit GI (57, medium) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for a lychee highball cocktail exists in any approved GI source. GL per 250 ml serving = 57 × 16.53 g/100g × 2.5 / 100 = 23.6 (high GL). This beverage contains alcohol and is not halal. The input record listed food_group as 'Mixed' and food_subgroup as 'Customisable mixed dishes'; reclassified to 'Beverages' and 'Alcoholic beverages — mixed cocktails' to better reflect the nature of this drink.
